skill.name: vercel-react-best-practices
skill.description: React and Next.js performance optimization guidelines from Vercel Engineering for AI agents
skill.summary: Comprehensive React and Next.js performance optimization rules covering bundle optimization, server-side caching, data fetching, and rendering performance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 911 pattern matches across 51 files (4901 lines), but all findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill is a documentation-only repository containing React/Next.js performance optimization guidelines in markdown format. Detected patterns (shell commands, dynamic imports, fetch calls, storage access) exist solely as code examples within documentation, not as executable code. No actual security risks identified.

content.value_statement: Build faster React and Next.js applications with proven performance patterns. This skill provides actionable guidelines from Vercel Engineering to eliminate waterfalls, reduce bundle size, and optimize rendering.
actual_capabilities: Identify and fix render waterfalls using Promise.all() and proper async patterns
Reduce bundle size with dynamic imports and tree-shaking optimization
Optimize server components with React.cache() and LRU caching strategies
Implement SWR for client-side data fetching with automatic deduplication
Fix unnecessary re-renders using useMemo, useCallback, and derived state patterns
Improve perceived performance with Suspense boundaries and streaming
limitations: Provides guidelines and code examples, not automatic code fixes
Focused on React 18+ and Next.js 13+ App Router patterns
Does not replace performance profiling tools like React DevTools
Examples use TypeScript; JavaScript-only projects may need adaptation

use_cases.title: Building new React features
use_cases.description: React developers building new features write components with optimal performance from the start, following Vercel-approved patterns for data fetching, rendering, and bundle optimization
use_cases.scenario: Writing new components with optimal performance from the start
use_cases.outcome: Components follow Vercel-approved patterns for data fetching, rendering, and bundle optimization

use_cases.title: Reviewing pull requests
use_cases.description: Teams reviewing pull requests check code for performance anti-patterns before merging, catching waterfalls, unnecessary re-renders, and bundle bloat early in development
use_cases.scenario: Checking code for performance anti-patterns before merging
use_cases.outcome: Catches waterfalls, unnecessary re-renders, and bundle bloat early in development

use_cases.title: Migrating to Next.js App Router
use_cases.description: Engineers migrating to Next.js App Router convert client-side patterns to server component architecture, learning proper use of server caching, serialization boundaries, and parallel data fetching
use_cases.user: Engineers migrating to Next.js App Router
use_cases.scenario: Converting client-side patterns to server component architecture
use_cases.outcome: Proper use of server caching, serialization boundaries, and parallel data fetching

prompt_templates.title: Review component for performance issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React component for performance issues. Identify any render waterfalls, unnecessary re-renders, or bundle optimization opportunities. Suggest specific improvements based on Vercel best practices.

prompt_templates.title: Fix data fetching waterfall
prompt_templates.prompt: This component fetches data sequentially. Refactor it to fetch in parallel using Promise.all() or restructure as server components. Explain which approach is better for this use case.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize server component caching
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this server component's data fetching. Recommend a caching strategy using React.cache() for per-request deduplication or LRU caching for cross-request optimization. Include cache invalidation considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Architect multi-page dashboard performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a dashboard with 10+ widgets showing real-time and historical data. Specify: 1) Server vs client component boundaries, 2) Data fetching and caching strategy, 3) Loading states with Suspense, 4) Bundle optimization for third-party charting libraries.

output_examples.output: Issue: Three useEffect hooks fetch data sequentially, causing render waterfall. Solution: Combine fetches into single useEffect with Promise.all() or convert parent to server component.

output_examples.input: Import statement pulling entire lodash library
output_examples.output: Issue: import _ from lodash adds 72KB to bundle. Solution: Use tree-shakeable imports like import debounce from lodash/debounce or switch to lodash-es for better bundler optimization.

output_examples.input: Component recalculating expensive value on every render
output_examples.output: Issue: Expensive computation runs on every render. Solution: Wrap in useMemo with proper dependencies, or extract to separate memoized component that only re-renders when inputs change.

best_practices: Start promises early and await late - initiate data fetching before it's needed, defer await until value is required
Use server components by default - only add 'use client' when browser APIs or interactivity are required
Prefer SWR or React Query for client data fetching - built-in caching, deduplication, and revalidation
anti_patterns: Sequential data fetching in separate useEffect hooks - causes render waterfalls
Importing entire utility libraries - bloated bundles, use specific imports instead
Using && for conditional rendering of components that render '0' - use ternary operator

faq.question: When should I use server components vs client components?
faq.answer: Default to server components for data fetching, heavy computation, and backend integrations. Use client components only for interactivity (useState, useEffect), browser APIs, or event handlers. Keep client components at leaf nodes of your component tree.

faq.question: How do I fix render waterfalls in my components?
faq.answer: Use Promise.all() for independent fetches, restructure to parallel fetching in server components, or use libraries like better-all for partial dependencies. Start fetches early (top-level) and await late (where needed).

faq.question: What's the difference between React.cache() and LRU caching?
faq.answer: React.cache() provides per-request deduplication - caches within a single render. LRU caching persists across requests using external stores like Redis or in-memory Map. Use cache() for deduplication, LRU for expensive cross-request data.

faq.question: How can I reduce my Next.js bundle size?
faq.answer: Use next/dynamic for heavy components, import utilities directly (not barrel files), defer third-party scripts until after hydration, and use conditional imports for features not needed on initial load. Check bundle analyzer with next-bundle-analyzer.

faq.question: Why does SWR help with client-side data fetching?
faq.answer: SWR provides automatic request deduplication (multiple components requesting same data share one fetch), caching with stale-while-revalidate strategy, focus revalidation, and optimistic updates. Reduces network requests and improves perceived performance.

faq.question: When should I use startTransition?
faq.answer: Use startTransition for non-urgent state updates that can be interrupted, like filtering large lists or typing in search inputs. Keeps UI responsive by allowing React to prioritize user interactions over background updates.
